title: Talauma oblongifolia
taxonomy: Talauma oblongifolia is classified under the family Magnoliaceae and belongs to the order Magnoliales. This classification places it within the broader group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The family Magnoliaceae is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the tropical regions of Asia. Talauma oblongifolia is further classified within the genus Talauma, which comprises various species of plants that are native to Southeast Asia. The classification of Talauma oblongifolia reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
uses: Talauma oblongifolia is known to have several medicinal uses, primarily in traditional Asian medicine. The plant's bark and leaves are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. Talauma oblongifolia is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery. Further research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and therapeutic applications.

intro: Talauma oblongifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Talauma oblongifolia include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Talauma oblongifolia is commonly used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and infusates, which are used to treat various ailments, as well as to make tonics and other herbal remedies.
